rulings.Case Title: FIDELITY NATIONAL TITLE COMPANY VS. ROBERT KEITH AMEETI ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 6, 2024 line 1. OTHER JOSEPH BABA MOTION TO INTERVENE is GRANTED. No opposition filed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TAYLOR DIXON VS. OLUSOJI A. FANOIKI
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING ON CLAIM OF RIGHT TO POSSESSION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 6, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANT FOLARIN DOMINGO HEARING ON CLAIM OF RIGHT TO POSSESSION Hearing Required. All evidence parties and claimants intend to present to the Court for consideration shall be filed/hard copies delivered to the Court by 9:30 a.m. on May 6, 2024. Such evidence shall include proof of rental payments (cancelled checks; bank statement's showing transfers; receipts); utility bills in claimant's name and/or utility payment receipts; copy of the rental agreement, etc. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: NAIFEH AZAR VS. PABLO E. RODRIGUEZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 6, 2024 line 5. DEFENDANT PABLO RODRIGUEZ, MARTHA RODRIGUEZ, EMMA VANEGAS, EMELINA SANCHEZ MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT Hearing at 10:30 a.m. Defendant's Motion to Strike Complaint is moot. The Court notes that the Notice of Motion states: "Plaintiff did not have leave of court to amend the First Amended Complaint and add these paragraphs." There is no First Amended Complaint on file in this action and the Motion to Strike addresses "Unlawful Detainer Complaint." =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
